sourceMovie.title: Source Code
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Source Code (2011) stuck because it commits to its thriller premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Duncan Jones's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The thriller and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Jacket
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Source Code clicked for you, The Jacket (2005) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Source Code?
faq.answer: Try The Jacket, Predestination, Déjà Vu—each shares DNA with Source Code while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Source Code?
faq.answer: Source Code (2011) is best described as Thriller, Science Fiction, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Source Code worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
